Today is a hard day for OpenSea, as we're letting go of ~20% of our team. Here's the note I shared with our team earlier this morning: https://twitter.com/...
OpenSea doing 20% layoffs. Accelerated equity vesting is super generous, though not sure who will buy. https://twitter.com/...
More startup layoffs hit the crypto space as @opensea, the world's largest NFT marketplace, lets go of ~20% of its workforce.
OpenSea, the largest NFT marketplace, is laying off 1 in 5 staffers. https://twitter.com/...
192 days ago, Opensea raised $300 million on a $13,000,000,000 valuation. https://twitter.com/...

The boom and bust cycle here gave me whiplash. Everything else aside for a second, this is a good reminder that the crazy speculations of the crypto universe, which can feel pretty abstract to say the least, impacts the lives of real human beings. https://twitter.com/...
OpenSea CEO @'s everyone in the channel to say we're laying off 20 percent of you https://www.vice.com/...
Official end of the NFT bubble? Perhaps the most intense and short-lived bubble in tech history? https://twitter.com/...

You can already see the edges around the USB-C ports in iJustine's video of the midnight Air turning silver... 😬 https://twitter.com/...

@rsgnl 💜 I've gone from being hopeful about getting them, to being skeptical, to being pessimistic about it ever happening, to just literally not even thinking about it haha. It's now entered “impossible” territory for me. Luckily I have a good audience that watches “late” reviews.
here's my review of the new MacBook Air with the M2 chip. it's an excellent computer, but costs a ton of money: https://www.theverge.com/... https://twitter.com/...
Which one would you rather buy? [pictures of $1,699 M2 MBA and $1.999 14" MBP]

Apple's M2-based MacBook Air gets a rare editor's choice from @danackerman for all-round utility. But the bare minimum I'd get — 16GB of RAM and 512GB SSD — costs $1600. This is not an entry-level machine, and the cheapest $1200 config also is pricey. https://www.cnet.com/...

The M2 MacBook Air having no branding on it whatsoever makes me think they debated at some point internally whether or not to call it just “MacBook” and slot it between the M1 Air and the 14-inch Pro.

@rsgnl @SnazzyQ Interesting, to whom has @Apple given review units to, so far for free (did they need to return it? How does sponsorship like that work?) God bless everyone. @MKBHD @ijustine @VadimYuryev @reneritchie @elonmusk @LinusTech @linusgsebastian
@brickguy6 @SnazzyQ Nah. To review an Apple product you must sign a loan agreement with a specified return date.

The M2 MacBook Air will be my new go-to recommendation when family/friends ask which Mac to buy — unless they want to spend the absolute minimum possible, in which case my recommendation is Apple's refurbished M1 MacBook Air for $849, which is still a great value. https://twitter.com/...
It's honestly a complete joke that Apple doesn't give review units to @SnazzyQ

Slightly disappointed that the new M2 MacBook Air doesn't have the “MacBook Air” branding stamped on the bottom unlike the 14"/16" MacBook Pro. (H/t to @dcseifert) https://twitter.com/... https://twitter.com/...
The 2022 version of Apple's MacBook Air gets a fresh look and the more powerful M2 processor, making it Apple's best ultraportable laptop yet. https://www.pcmag.com/...
The midnight MacBook Air comes with MIDNIGHT APPLE STICKERS! Via @Dave2D https://twitter.com/...
So many people conflate the original MacBook Air with the 2010 redesign. They are not even remotely the same.

Apple's new MacBook Air M2 may not have gotten the same pomp and circumstance other computers have, but it's already won a @CNET Editor's Choice award. “Apple has succeeded in nailing that formula more often than not,” says @danackerman https://www.cnet.com/...

I'm not jumping to any extremes here. The switch to a single NAND chip in the base 256GB model is a reason to avoid the base model if you want the fastest SSD speeds possible. Simple. I'm not saying oh my gosh the M2 MacBook Air is trash — it seems like a pretty awesome machine.

Apple: “While benchmarks of the 256GB SSD may show a difference compared to the previous generation, the performance of these M2 based systems for real world activities are even faster.” This is vague. Sounds like Apple is just saying the M2 chip is faster *overall* as PR spin.
There is a difference between saying “the performance of these M2 based systems for real world activities are even faster” and “the performance of the SSD for real world activities is even faster.”
Looks like Apple sent people the 512GB M2 MacBook Airs instead of 256GB ones, are they acknowledging that the Base Model has worse SSD Performance? 😂 @VadimYuryev What're you guys thinking?

Also, Apple told me it would keep me in mind for an M2 MacBook Air review unit. Narrator: didn't happen. But I will never shy away from simply reporting the facts regardless. I'm not for or against Apple. I have no agenda. I try to inform people of the facts.

Me: The M2 MacBook Pro severe thermal throttling makes me worried about the M2 Air. Others: “You're wrong! You Apple hater! You're being too negative! M2 Air will be fine.” M2 Air: 🔥🔥🔥 25% performance drop in 30 min loop. Screenshot from @verge https://twitter.com/...
M2 MacBook Air Review: Appealing Inside and Out https://gizmodo.com/... https://twitter.com/...
My MacBook Air M2 review is live. And it's the best laptop period. https://www.tomsguide.com/... https://twitter.com/...

My M2 MacBook Air review on @inputmag Apple knocked it out of the park with this laptop. The new design, M2 chip performance, battery life — all *chef's kiss* I also included some nerdy FCP export times. Enjoy! https://www.inputmag.com/...

Honestly, it kinda sucks that the new M2 MacBook Air looks like an old 13-inch MacBook Pro. The old Air design was so iconic. Well, at least the new midnight color looks great. https://twitter.com/...

i don't think the 13-inch MacBook Pro or the base model M2 Air should exist. so when you ignore those as you should, Apple's laptop line up clears up a bit: $1000: M1 Air $1500: M2 Air $2000: MBP 14 $2500: MBP 16 and then you configure w/in those tiers per prefs/needs
MacBook Air with M2 Review: Modern Design + Modern Performance https://www.thestreet.com/... @TheStreet #MacBookAir #M2 #Future #Review https://twitter.com/...

Macbook Air M2's most notable feature is... the smudges? ;) https://t.co/MSE3ygDPB4 https://t.co/Rx4n16gEcZ
So glad Twitter is back. Now you can read our deep Apple MacBook Air (M2, 2022) review! https://www.techradar.com/...

@stevesi The commenters aren't wrong. Apple PR would never pay money for reviews but there's lots of ways to influence the influencers. Access to hardware and execs translate directly to the bottom line for those blessed.

Ok everybody, it's time to talk about the new M2 MacBook Air! First, here's the full review on @CNET, with performance charts. It does so much right, but also one thing I really don't like... 1/x https://www.cnet.com/...
Apple MacBook Air With M2 Chip Review: Lightweight Build and Supercharged Performance https://hypebeast.com/...
This is what I suspected. If they're having trouble cooling the M2 with the old MacBook Pro design, how could it ever work in an even thinner, fan-less chassis? https://twitter.com/...
So the MacBook Air M2 is pretty good https://www.engadget.com/...
The M2 MacBook Air seems great overall, but ⁦@dcseifert⁩ found the M2 performance story is complicated https://www.theverge.com/...

The new M2 chip was...moderately faster than the M1. But the M2 is nowhere near as powerful as the M1 Pro, and M1 Max. The Apple Silicon chip naming conventions are already confusing and we're only on the second generation. https://twitter.com/...
The M2 MacBook Air review: A new era that's just right https://www.macworld.com/...

Dave2D on the M2 MacBook Air SSD issue: “I think if they're gonna have slower drive speeds on those entry-level models, they need to disclose that. It should be on the website.....Because that's just information people need to know.” Yes, Apple.. Please! https://www.youtube.com/...
MacBook Air M2 review (2022): Apple's near-perfect Mac https://www.engadget.com/... https://twitter.com/...
here's the thing though before this turns into a big week-long controversy: if you regularly have intense workloads, you're not buying a MacBook Air. https://twitter.com/...

Reviews of the new MacBook Air with the M2 chip are out and @verge has confirmed that the base model with 256GB of storage has slower SSD speeds compared to the equivalent model with the M1 chip. Just like the 13-inch MacBook Pro with the M2 chip. https://twitter.com/...
@stevesi There are those who don't engage like that, others who do if but really badly and As Apple that's at the apex and refined the art to new levels.
@Gartenberg The thing is everyone does that so it is a level playing field in that respect. There's no unfair advantage.
It's an new era for the MacBook Air, but the M2 model is a fine successor to a classic design. My review: https://sixcolors.com/...
The new MacBook Air with M2 has a great new display and webcam. MagSafe 3 is back. Here's our review: https://tomshardware.com/... https://twitter.com/...
M2 MacBook Air reviews are in: new era, all-day battery, same base storage concern as MacBook Pro https://9to5mac.com/... by @joseadorno

Not doing reviews right now, but a ton of friends have their M2 MBA videos live! 1/3 @iJustine https://www.youtube.com/... @Dave2D https://www.youtube.com/... @lora_krystal https://www.youtube.com/... @briantong https://www.youtube.com/... @AndruEdwards https://www.youtube.com/...
Yeah people buying this arent being productive or putting workloads on it, this is for FaceTime/kids to play games on https://twitter.com/...
Very non-Apple industrial design issue. But then again, different finishes are a bit new to Apple. I wonder if @ijustine's unit was a preproduction unit. $AAPL https://twitter.com/...
Is it my imagination, or have the reviews of Apple's new M2-based MBA been less than stellar? https://twitter.com/...
Apple's product naming is going great, as usual. [Screen cap from Verge review with “M2 Pro's” highlighted]
M2 MacBook Air Reviews: 'Apple's Near-Perfect Mac' https://www.macrumors.com/... // don't miss the comments!
512GB model it is for recommendations! Welcome to the $1399 MacBook Air. https://twitter.com/...

Apple's new M2 MacBook Air is the start of a whole new Air-a, says @dcseifert. It has a much-improved screen and camera, and solid battery life / performance. Full review here: https://www.theverge.com/... https://twitter.com/...
Two M2 MacBook Air videos that I really enjoyed this morning: @Dave2D: https://www.youtube.com/... @iJustine: https://www.youtube.com/... Check them out.

Sources: Stripe, last valued by private investors at $95B, told staff it cut its internal share price by 28%, lowering those shares' implied valuation to $74B

Stripe, the payments start-up, lowers internal valuation 28 percent.

WSJ EXCLUSIVE from me: Stripe cuts internal valuation by 28%, giving it an implied valuation of $74 billion (this is a 409a valuation used to price employee stock options, separate from investor shares) https://www.wsj.com/...

The real issue with private market valuation & late stage in particular right now is how much has valuations stumbled. Had Stripe IPOed would it be only 28% down? And let's remember Stripe had more than $12 billion gross revenue last year & profitable. https://www.wsj.com/...
If Stripe is slashing their valuation but you didn't, you are in trouble. https://www.wsj.com/...
Best wishes to those who bought Stripe shares at $190b valuation in the secondary market this March, and $150b last year. https://twitter.com/... https://twitter.com/...
WSJ EXCLUSIVE: Payments giant Stripe, last valued by private investors at $95 billion, has cut the internal value of its shares by 28% https://www.wsj.com/...
28%. Why bless their heart that's so cute. https://www.wsj.com/...
Still way too high stripe is worth nowhere near $74b let them ipo and find out https://twitter.com/...

Regulators should crack down on crypto, after it gained an illusion of respectability through association with high-status institutions and individuals

Every time a student asks me my thoughts on #Crypto, I've always started with “total scam” or “it's not actually real and doesn't have intrinsic value”. @paulkrugman's label “pyramid scheme” is probably the most apt description I've seen. @nytimes https://www.nytimes.com/...
Crypto is a predatory junkyard. Regulate it. https://www.nytimes.com/... #Crypto #cryptocurrency #cryptocurrencies

The degree to which universities and professors acted as a reputation laundering service for crypto scams is vastly under-reported. That's how this scam gained “legitimacy” and then used it to defraud the public https://twitter.com/...
Krugman panning crypto: this is the strongest long-term bull signal possible. https://www.nytimes.com/...
In which Paul Krugman passive aggressively implies his friend Larry Summers is on the take from crypto bros. https://www.nytimes.com/...
“Other than that, it's all good” https://www.nytimes.com/...

Tradit'l banking is regulated for a reason; crypto has created an environment subject to bank runs, not to mention “theft, hacks and ransom attacks” — plus “money laundering and financing of terrorism.” Other than that, it's all good. https://nytimes.com/...

Exactly 1 yr ago (in @TorontoStar) I argued crypto had absolutely zero economic value, would inevitably collapse, and regulators needed to do their job: https://www.thestar.com/.... No fun saying “I told you so” when people's lives are destroyed. Pierre Poilievre aided & abetted it. https://twitter.com/...

“But what about all the mostly youngish, not especially rich people who bought crypto because Venmo was flacking it?” @PaulKrugman responded to readers commenting on his column “Crypto Is Crashing. Where Were the Regulators?” Join the conversation: https://www.nytimes.com/... https://twitter.com/...
“The way I see it, crypto evolved into a sort of postmodern pyramid scheme. The industry lured investors in with a combination of technobabble and libertarian derp” https://www.nytimes.com/...

I love that when crypto was cool, *very smart people* praised it for existing outside of governments because “this was going to change everything.” Now that it's bleeding out *very smart people* are asking “where were the regulators?” https://twitter.com/...

Krugman strikes again, like lightening “The way I see it, crypto evolved into a sort of postmodern pyramid scheme. The industry lured investors in with a combination of technobabble and libertarian derp” https://www.nytimes.com/...
Another industry that has “collaborated” a lot with academics is crypto. I suspect the sums involved are orders of magnitude higher than Uber's lobbying funds. https://twitter.com/...

the $TWTR deal is precisely what lead Elliott to $PINS they suckered Musk into paying ~7.5x EV/Revenue for Twitter (hardly makes any money), in the process realized Pinterest (comp to $TWTR $SNAP $META) was a better business trading at ~40% of Twitter's pro-forma M&A multiple.

Los Angeles-based Merkle Manufactory, which is developing a “sufficiently decentralized” social network protocol called Farcaster, has raised $30M led by a16z
Farcaster  —  Two years ago, Varun and I started working together on an idea called RSS+.
U.S. Inflation at 9.1%, Farcaster's Successful in a Funding Round, Crypto Market Bearish Spell Continues
Excited to share an update about @farcaster_xyz! https://danromero.org/... https://twitter.com/...
I like how casual @pmarca is on @farcaster_xyz 😂 https://twitter.com/...

There is no doubt good builders will always attract the capital they need. Even in “bear” markets. @farcaster_xyz just raised a $30m seed. From @a16z, @multicoincap, @mickymalka, @eladgil, @VoltCapital, @coinbase, @1confirmation, @standardcrypto, @firstround, @balajis and more

We're excited to start building @bebcaster, a group platform built on top of the @bebdotxyz and @farcaster_xyz protocols for Farcaster-only communities. We'll start “off-farcaster” due to farcaster v1 limitations, and as v2 evolves, we'll move more things “on-farcaster”! 🏗️ https://twitter.com/...
it's a privilege to be a small contributor to Farcaster's round. @dwr, @varunsrin and the team are legends and the social protocol of the future gets better every day :) https://twitter.com/...
Excited to be supporting Farcaster as a small angel and daily user! https://twitter.com/... https://twitter.com/...

I use this more than twitter, and some really cool stuff has already been built on top of it! e.g. https://instacaster.xyz/ and https://www.castrss.xyz/ (yes lol RSS still works! i get a text for updates). I have some ideas cooking as well... https://twitter.com/...
FC has replaced most of my twitter usage already. So excited for this! https://twitter.com/...
It's rare to find a compelling new social network for the web3 community and this is one of the best I've seen. Proud to back @farcaster_xyz! https://twitter.com/...

The best part about Farcaster (aside from the community) is that you'd never know that it's decentralized — truly the future of web3. Instead of focusing on maximal decentralization, Dan and Varun are focused on sufficient decentralization. https://twitter.com/...

Farcaster is a wonderful community, and Varun and Dan are very thoughtful founders. Lots of people talk in the web3, but this crew has been steadily building. Highly recommend taking a look if you haven't yet. https://twitter.com/...

Farcaster rn feels like early clubhouse. Except,,, it's a protocol. Let a thousand early clubhouses bloom on top of it. folks already building on top: https://castrss.xyz/ by @shawkisukkar https://instacaster.xyz/ by @zachterrell57 https://searchcaster.xyz/ by @gregskril https://twitter.com/...

We have been looking far and wide for a web3 social project with technology architecture that is credibly neutral with the hope of building a healthier internet. @dwr and team at @farcaster_xyz have built something new and important. @offlineventures is excited to be on board. https://twitter.com/...

Ecstatic about the opportunity to work with the @farcaster_xyz team. The decentralization of layers of the tech stack above the base blockchain/smart contract layer is going to open up a new world of possibilities for web3 products and services, including web3 social media. https://twitter.com/...
I remember the excitement around https://app.net/ when it launched Wonder what is possible now that ethereum (and, more importantly, the ethereum community) exists https://twitter.com/...
Farcaster is my favorite platform and community on the internet - can't wait to continue working with the awesome @dwr and @varunsrin https://twitter.com/...

Very excited to partner with Dan and Varun. Been an avid user of Farcaster for a while, and especially excited about all the different ways the product and ecosystem can develop. More below 👇 https://twitter.com/...

Farcaster has raised $30M in a round led by Andreessen Horowitz (a16z) @farcaster_xyz Investors: @a16z, @Coinbase, @multicoincap, @1confirmation, @scalarcapital, @firstround, #ACapital, @offlineventures, @archetype, @CanonicalCrypto 👉 https://theblock.co/... https://twitter.com/...

🚨👋 We are thrilled to partner with @dwr and @varunsrin as they build @farcaster_xyz. They're building something very exciting around web3/decentralized social networks and it has quickly become something I personally use everyday. PS - they're hiring! https://twitter.com/...

Andrej Karpathy, exec who led Tesla's Autopilot efforts, resigns

It's been a great pleasure to help Tesla towards its goals over the last 5 years and a difficult decision to part ways. In that time, Autopilot graduated from lane keeping to city streets and I look forward to seeing the exceptionally strong Autopilot team continue that momentum.
@karpathy Thanks for everything you have done for Tesla! It has been an honor working with you.
I have no concrete plans for what's next but look to spend more time revisiting my long-term passions around technical work in AI, open source and education.

My take on the state of @Tesla FSD: 1/ very hard problem 2/ higher than human expectations 3/ underpowered silicon for expectations 4/ mistake using camera-only without radar or LiDAR https://www.cnbc.com/... $TSLA

Karpathy said it best. From the time he started Tesla to today they've gone from basic lane keeping to full city streets driving.
